jokes.text: Oh, loving it. Want another one? - Yeah, I do. - I'm really sorry. - I'm really, really, really sorry. - Now sing it.
jokes.setup: Mike apologizing profusely for some past mistake
jokes.punchline: The other person sadistically enjoying making Mike grovel more and more
jokes.explanation: Well-structured escalation with clear sadistic pleasure taken in humiliation. Good economy and character work.

jokes.text: Even Wall Street's got a boner for you guys. - Really? The market's up? - No, it's flat. But not down is straight up for us at this point.
jokes.setup: Excited news about Wall Street having a 'boner' for the president
jokes.punchline: Market is actually flat, but their standards are so low that 'not down' counts as success
jokes.explanation: Perfect structure with expectation setup and brilliant subversion. The pathetic lowering of standards is perfectly Veep.

jokes.text: I got a great idea. Why don't we give the Chinese their own log-ins and passwords? Okay? Save everybody a lot of time.
jokes.setup: Chinese hackers have breached National Security Council servers
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically suggesting they just give hackers direct access
jokes.explanation: Good Selina-style sarcastic response to crisis. Strong economy and character voice.

jokes.text: - Got a few fowl questions. - What?
jokes.setup: FDA monitoring salmonella outbreak at chicken farm
jokes.punchline: 'Fowl questions' pun completely missed by Selina
jokes.explanation: Simple pun that falls flat - the reaction beat of confusion is more funny than the pun itself.

jokes.text: Charlie says O'Brien won't go down on his wife without biscuits and gravy.
jokes.setup: Selina sharing intimate gossip about political rival
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and gross sexual detail
jokes.explanation: Perfectly disgusting and specific detail that's both shocking and character-appropriate for Selina's oversharing.

jokes.text: I mean, seriously, do we have any reason to think that we're not as fucked as a Senate page here?
jokes.setup: Asking about their election chances
jokes.punchline: Reference to Senate pages being sexually exploited by senators
jokes.explanation: Dark political humor referencing real scandal. Good structure but relies on external reference.

jokes.text: Christ, Kent, know your audience.
jokes.setup: Kent using statistical jargon about Nevada voting data
jokes.punchline: Exasperated response to his overly technical explanation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Kent's inability to communicate normally, but fairly standard reaction.

jokes.text: Hey, Garfield, we're right in the middle of talking about Nevada, okay?
jokes.setup: Someone interrupting serious Nevada discussion with Vanity Fair photo request
jokes.punchline: Dismissive 'Garfield' nickname for the interrupting staffer
jokes.explanation: Good dismissive nickname and timing. Shows character priorities and irritation well.

jokes.text: What Alan Turing is trying to say is if there are missing votes, he needs time to find them.
jokes.setup: Kent's overly complex statistical explanation
jokes.punchline: Comparing Kent to famous mathematician Alan Turing
jokes.explanation: Smart reference that perfectly captures Kent's overly intellectual approach. Good character specificity.

jokes.text: And after I'm done dick slapping him, he will have agreed to delay certification.
jokes.setup: Bob offering to meet with someone to negotiate
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor for aggressive negotiation tactics
jokes.explanation: Vivid crude metaphor that's both shocking and character-appropriate for Bob's aggressive style.

jokes.text: - Amy, set it up. - The injunction or the dick slapping?
jokes.setup: Selina asking Amy to set up the meeting after Bob's crude comment
jokes.punchline: Amy's deadpan clarification about which task to arrange
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan callback with excellent timing and economy. Amy's straight delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I just... I feel trapped like a Saudi housekeeper.
jokes.setup: Selina complaining about feeling trapped in DC
jokes.punchline: Extremely dark comparison to human rights abuse
jokes.explanation: Shockingly inappropriate comparison that perfectly captures Selina's self-centered worldview and lack of awareness.

jokes.text: Oh, come on, please, Bob, I would never stay at Caesar's.
jokes.setup: Bob saying she could piss in Caesar's fountain if she wins
jokes.punchline: Selina's focus on the hotel choice rather than the crude act
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection showing Selina's elitist priorities. She's more concerned about hotel prestige than public urination.

jokes.text: Catherine, do not use any of the vulgar parts. - Yeah, but that's like all of it, Mom.
jokes.setup: Selina telling daughter not to use vulgar parts for documentary
jokes.punchline: Catherine pointing out that eliminates everything
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about the reality of their administration. Catherine's matter-of-fact delivery works well.

jokes.text: Dan, Sophie doesn't work for CBS. She works for CVS. You sold your dick for bulk iced tea and off-brand cough syrup.
jokes.setup: Dan thinking Sophie works for CBS network
jokes.punchline: Revelation that CVS is the pharmacy chain, making his conquest pathetic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup and payoff with devastating specificity. The 'bulk iced tea and off-brand cough syrup' detail is perfect.

jokes.text: Don't worry. You're gonna look really cute in a blue vest.
jokes.setup: Previous CVS revelation
jokes.punchline: Reference to CVS employee uniform, further humiliating Dan
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that extends the humiliation with specific visual detail of him working retail.

jokes.text: I am not having a good year.
jokes.setup: Dan's series of professional and personal humiliations
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of his terrible situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect understated reaction that lets the audience fill in how catastrophic his year has been.

jokes.text: Because they're a sneaky bunch of fucks.
jokes.setup: CIA briefing about captured prisoner, asking about Israeli involvement
jokes.punchline: Casual anti-Semitic characterization of allies
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's inappropriate casual prejudice and lack of diplomatic filter during security briefing.

jokes.text: Sorry, Kent, Salt and Moon, new restaurant.
jokes.setup: Mike interrupting serious CIA briefing to take restaurant call
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate priority during national security discussion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike moment showing his complete lack of situational awareness and misplaced priorities.

jokes.text: Well, Sue uses my name for POTUS reservations, so when I call, I get an amazing table.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he got reservation at exclusive restaurant
jokes.punchline: Abusing presidential access for personal dining
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mike's casual corruption and petty abuse of power for restaurant access.

jokes.text: Please, Marjorie, please. I have to talk to the president. - Please take a step back.
jokes.setup: Mike desperately trying to get to the president about Twitter crisis
jokes.punchline: Secret Service physically blocking him despite the emergency
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of bureaucratic barriers during crisis. Mike's desperation versus inflexible security.

jokes.text: Hi, ma'am. If you hear this, do not tweet. - Mike, what are you doing?
jokes.setup: Mike desperately trying to communicate through door during CIA briefing
jokes.punchline: Awkward interruption of serious meeting with social media crisis
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing the chaos of modern political communication crises interrupting everything.

jokes.text: Hi, ma'am. If you hear this, do not tweet. / Please, stop... ma'am?
jokes.setup: Gary desperately trying to communicate through glass/door
jokes.punchline: Pathetic attempt to prevent social media disaster
jokes.explanation: Gary's helpless desperation as disaster unfolds

jokes.text: I just direct messaged Charlie. - No, it was public. Everybody saw it on Twitter.
jokes.setup: Selina thinking she sent private message
jokes.punchline: Revelation that embarrassing content went public
jokes.explanation: Classic tech confusion disaster. Selina's ignorance of social media creates public embarrassment.

jokes.text: I pushed that feather button. - I know, you tweet... that's a tweet.
jokes.setup: Selina explaining what she did
jokes.punchline: Her calling the Twitter bird logo a 'feather button'
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail showing Selina's tech illiteracy. 'Feather button' is a brilliantly specific misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Gary, I did it the way you showed me to do it. - But that's not the way I showed you to do... - That's exactly the way that you showed me to do it.
jokes.setup: Gary trying to explain Twitter to Selina
jokes.punchline: Selina's stubborn insistence she followed instructions perfectly
jokes.explanation: Good back-and-forth showing Selina's refusal to admit error and Gary's frustrated attempts to help.

jokes.text: You better not.
jokes.setup: Gary offering to make tea as consolation
jokes.punchline: Selina's petty extension of his punishment to include tea-making
jokes.explanation: Perfect petty escalation showing Selina's vindictive nature extending to preventing basic kindness.

jokes.text: Well, what the FOL are we supposed to do about this?
jokes.setup: Swedish PM retweeted with 'LOL'
jokes.punchline: Selina's attempt at wordplay with 'FOL' (Fuck Out Loud)
jokes.explanation: Selina's attempt at internet slang humor shows her trying to be hip while being completely out of touch.

jokes.text: Phones are hacked all the time to steal pictures of celebrities' penises and whatnot.
jokes.setup: Suggesting they claim Selina's phone was hacked
jokes.punchline: Kent's clinical reference to celebrity nude photo leaks
jokes.explanation: Kent's matter-of-fact delivery of crude content in clinical language. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Maybe the firewalls have more holes in them than a gold digger's diaphragm.
jokes.setup: Discussing cybersecurity vulnerabilities
jokes.punchline: Crude analogy comparing firewalls to contraceptive failures
jokes.explanation: Vivid and shocking analogy that works both technically and crudely. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: My God, I hated those things.
jokes.setup: Previous diaphragm reference
jokes.punchline: Selina's inappropriate personal overshare about contraception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina overshare moment. Her complete lack of filter in professional settings is character-defining.

jokes.text: Oh, no, ma'am, no. You have no idea what happened. And neither does Mike, who would be outraged by this.
jokes.setup: Coaching Selina on plausible deniability for Twitter incident
jokes.punchline: Explicitly coaching her to claim ignorance while Mike stands right there
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on political coaching and manufactured outrage. The transparency is part of the joke.

jokes.text: Actually, ma'am, I have a better idea. - No, you don't, Mike. History has proven that.
jokes.setup: Mike offering to contribute an idea
jokes.punchline: Immediate brutal shutdown citing his track record
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate shutdown with devastating specificity. 'History has proven that' is brutally definitive.

jokes.text: It's been the cornerstone of American foreign policy since the Spanish American War.
jokes.setup: Selina asking if she can really blame another country for something they didn't do
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact explanation that this is standard American practice
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire with historical specificity. Dark but accurate observation about foreign policy.

jokes.text: And yet he's responding.
jokes.setup: O'Brien saying he won't dignify remarks with a response
jokes.punchline: Pointing out he's literally responding while saying he won't respond
jokes.explanation: Good observation of political hypocrisy. Simple but effective pointing out of logical contradiction.

jokes.text: You're the one who suggested we all live in the Matrix. - Ma'am, nobody chose to live in the Matrix.
jokes.setup: Selina referencing Ben's Matrix comparison
jokes.punchline: Ben's pedantic correction about Matrix plot details
jokes.explanation: Good nerd humor with Ben being pedantically correct about sci-fi details during crisis.

jokes.text: Whose side were you on?
jokes.setup: Ben explaining Matrix plot about machines using humans
jokes.punchline: Selina asking which side he supported in fictional scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - treating fictional movie plot as real political issue requiring position-taking.

jokes.text: Have them what? Have them help us park in front of a fire hydrant?
jokes.setup: Someone suggesting going to UN for help
jokes.punchline: Selina's dismissive comparison to useless parking assistance
jokes.explanation: Good dismissive analogy showing contempt for UN effectiveness with specific mundane comparison.

jokes.text: I don't know why. My only jobs seem to be asking Bob and finding out what Bob thinks.
jokes.setup: Amy explaining why she's still around
jokes.punchline: Bitter summary of how her role has been diminished
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Amy's frustrated awareness of her marginalization in the campaign.

jokes.text: Oh, but on your way, would you mind stopping at ABC News and picking up some Advil? Oh, did I say ABC News? I meant Rite Aid.
jokes.setup: Pretending to confuse news network with pharmacy
jokes.punchline: Mocking Dan's earlier CBS/CVS confusion
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure deliberately echoing Dan's embarrassing mistake to continue mocking him.

jokes.text: Good night, have a pleasant evening, and I had sex with your sister.
jokes.setup: Dan trying to leave with dignity
jokes.punchline: Repeating the sister comment as if it's a polite goodbye
jokes.explanation: Good absurd formality mixed with crude personal attack. Dan's attempt at dignity while being petty.

jokes.text: You might actually want to go to Rite Aid. Get some Valtrex.
jokes.setup: Dan's sister comment
jokes.punchline: Amy suggesting he needs herpes medication
jokes.explanation: Good comeback implying Dan's sexual health issues. Specific brand name makes it more effective.

jokes.text: You might actually want to go to Rite Aid. Get some Valtrex.
jokes.setup: Dan's repeated mention of sleeping with her sister
jokes.punchline: Amy suggesting he get STD medication
jokes.explanation: Perfect comeback that turns Dan's boast into concern about STDs

jokes.text: No, no, that's impossible 'cause he's Mike McLintock
jokes.setup: Told McLintock party already seated
jokes.punchline: Companion's confused insistence about Mike's identity
jokes.explanation: Sets up the restaurant disaster by showing Mike's identity confusion

jokes.text: did you shave it off of Freddie Mercury's corpse?
jokes.setup: Continuing mustache insults
jokes.punchline: Reference to dead celebrity's facial hair
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that crosses into bad taste without much comedic payoff

jokes.text: This is my fat johnson and you can suck it as long as you promise not to put your dirty gay mustache on it.
jokes.setup: Mike's table being taken by someone else
jokes.punchline: Shockingly crude and offensive response from table thief
jokes.explanation: Crude shock humor that relies more on offensive content than craft. Creates uncomfortable atmosphere effectively.

jokes.text: Jesus, you think I married that?
jokes.setup: Mike's date being called Will's wife
jokes.punchline: Will's cruel dismissal of his own wife
jokes.explanation: Reveals Will's horrible character through casual cruelty to spouse

jokes.text: Christ, she looks like a Batman villain.
jokes.setup: Seeing someone's wife
jokes.punchline: Specific visual comparison to comic book villain
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective visual insult with specific pop culture reference that creates clear image.

jokes.text: Does a bear piss in the shower?
jokes.setup: Selina asking if her instincts are right
jokes.punchline: Bob's garbled version of 'Does a bear shit in the woods?'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bob's mental state through mangled common phrase. Foreshadows his decline.

jokes.text: Yes! This is better than phone sex. - Well, I just came.
jokes.setup: Selina getting excited about Bob's political advice
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate sexual metaphor for political strategy excitement
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate sexual escalation of political excitement. Crude but captures the power dynamic well.

jokes.text: One heck of a job, Amy.
jokes.setup: Selina praising Amy and asking Ben to confirm
jokes.punchline: Ben's obviously insincere, minimal praise
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery showing Ben's reluctance to genuinely praise Amy. Minimal effort compliance.

jokes.text: Those things cause cancer, you know
jokes.setup: Checking if cell phones are off
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of cancer risk
jokes.explanation: Throwaway line about cell phone fears delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: What we have here is a classic thumb in the apple pie situation and Grandma is mad as hell. She spent all day cooking...
jokes.setup: Bob trying to negotiate with political metaphor
jokes.punchline: Increasingly nonsensical folksy analogy that goes nowhere
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Bob's mental decline through increasingly incoherent folksy metaphors.

jokes.text: We all know you're as folksy as a butter churn lamp, but let me cut to the chase.
jokes.setup: Bob's folksy approach
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd comparison to country kitsch item
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific visual metaphor for fake folksy personality. 'Butter churn lamp' is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: there's never been a Whitman's Sampler I couldn't take apart piece by piece and shit out, Madam President.
jokes.setup: Bob heading to negotiate with Whitman
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor comparing negotiation to consuming candy
jokes.explanation: Good crude confidence with specific brand reference. Shows Bob's deteriorating filter and judgment.

jokes.text: there's never been a Whitman's Sampler I couldn't take apart piece by piece and shit out
jokes.setup: Bob going to Whitman meeting
jokes.punchline: Crude metaphor comparing negotiation to eating/defecating candy
jokes.explanation: Creative play on Whitman's name with characteristically crude Bob confidence

jokes.text: A lady president? Yeah, maybe in the year 2000.
jokes.setup: Richard trying to get Bob's attention
jokes.punchline: Bob's confused response mixing current reality with past time
jokes.explanation: Excellent confusion showing Bob's cognitive decline. He's forgotten current reality and timeline.

jokes.text: Ned Mitchell died in 2006. And the 'Las Vegas Gazette' stopped publishing in 2007.
jokes.setup: Bob trying to set up media contacts
jokes.punchline: Revelation that his contacts are long dead/defunct
jokes.explanation: Perfect reveal of Bob's disconnect from reality. Specific dates make the cognitive decline concrete.

jokes.text: which is not a newspaper, it's a legal brothel. But she did seem excited to help the campaign in whichever way she could.
jokes.setup: Jonah managed to contact Susan Bailey at 'Reno Star'
jokes.punchline: It's a brothel, but the sex worker is enthusiastic about helping
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect timing. The enthusiasm for campaign help makes it funnier.

jokes.text: which is not a newspaper, it's a legal brothel. But she did seem excited to help the campaign in whichever way she could
jokes.setup: Jonah contacted Susan Bailey at 'Reno Star'
jokes.punchline: It's a brothel, but the prostitute is eager to help politically
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with perfect innuendo about campaign 'help' from sex worker

jokes.text: Ma'am, at his age, if he can take a shit whenever he wants, that's a major victory.
jokes.setup: Worry about Bob embarrassing himself in meeting
jokes.punchline: Reframing bowel control as an achievement for elderly people
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about aging with practical medical reality. Reframes the concern effectively.

jokes.text: this meeting cannot make it past the School Book Depository. I'm loading my gun and heading to the sixth floor.
jokes.setup: Amy saying she needs to stop the meeting
jokes.punchline: JFK assassination metaphor for stopping Bob
jokes.explanation: Dark historical reference that's both specific and dramatically appropriate for the crisis situation.

jokes.text: Alzheimer's or no, I image-searched Susan Bailey and she is legit fuckable.
jokes.setup: Previous brothel revelation
jokes.punchline: Jonah's inappropriate research and assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah moment showing his inappropriate priorities and lack of filter during crisis.

jokes.text: This is like that famous scene from the movie 'Heat.' You know, with De Niro and Brenneman. Just thinking about that scene gives me goose bumps
jokes.setup: High-stakes car chase to stop political disaster
jokes.punchline: Richard's excited movie comparison to completely wrong film
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard moment - getting movie references wrong while being enthusiastic

jokes.text: This is like that famous scene from the movie 'Heat.' You know, with De Niro and Brenneman. Just thinking about that scene gives me goose bumps.
jokes.setup: Driving quickly to stop Bob
jokes.punchline: Richard's inappropriate movie reference to action film
jokes.explanation: Good Richard character moment showing his tendency to reference movies inappropriately during serious situations.

jokes.text: Well, hopefully she arrives before we all turn into fossils.
jokes.setup: Charlie making speech about Selina being late to museum event
jokes.punchline: Fossil joke at Natural History Museum while waiting for president
jokes.explanation: Good situational humor playing on museum setting and Selina's chronic lateness. Well-crafted.

jokes.text: Yeah, turns out he's nuttier than a squirrel's diaper.
jokes.setup: Explaining Bob's mental state
jokes.punchline: Absurd analogy combining 'nuts' pun with impossible scenario
jokes.explanation: Creative absurd analogy with good wordplay. 'Squirrel's diaper' is perfectly ridiculous mental image.

jokes.text: Sometimes up to 16% end up missing or thrown out. - Is that what this is about? - The missing ballots in Washoe County.
jokes.setup: Richard explaining ballot irregularities in general terms
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing exactly what they're looking for
jokes.explanation: Perfect accidental revelation. Richard's incompetence creates dramatic irony as he gives away their strategy.

jokes.text: Funny thing about elections, historically there've always been leftover ballots. Sometimes up to 16% end up missing or thrown out
jokes.setup: Richard apparently making small talk about elections
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals key information about missing ballots
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Richard moment - accidentally solving the case through clueless trivia sharing

jokes.text: Holy bacon double Asperger's.
jokes.setup: Richard realizing he accidentally solved the case
jokes.punchline: Bizarre exclamation combining food and disability reference
jokes.explanation: Richard's characteristic weird exclamation but relies on disability reference for shock value rather than craft.

jokes.text: And you can't say 'Meet the Parents' 'cause that's automatically number one.
jokes.setup: Asking Amy about favorite De Niro movies
jokes.punchline: Assuming terrible De Niro comedy is automatically the best
jokes.explanation: Good film snob humor about mainstream versus prestige movies. Character-appropriate movie geekery.

jokes.text: She's a fucking cunt. - You didn't hear it from me.
jokes.setup: Someone mentioning Queen of England story
jokes.punchline: Selina's shocking assessment of British monarch
jokes.explanation: Shocking diplomatic impropriety that's perfectly in character. The casual delivery makes it more effective.

jokes.text: In many cultures, the word vote can mean a myriad of things. It can mean a celebration. It can mean a dance around a fire. It can mean a silent nod.
jokes.setup: Lawyer trying to stall court by defining 'vote'
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous anthropological definitions
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist stalling technique with escalating ridiculous cultural interpretations. Well-structured escalation.

jokes.text: What kind of soup do you have today? - Um, what kind of soup is on your mind? - Well, what is today? - Um... it's Thursday. - Thursday? - Yup. - Navy bean. So delicious.
jokes.setup: Finding confused Bob at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Bob's confused interaction showing complete disconnection from reality
jokes.explanation: Heartbreaking character moment showing Bob's complete cognitive disconnect. The confused logic is perfectly written.

jokes.text: Bob Bradley. Nice to meet you, sir.
jokes.setup: Bob being found by his colleagues
jokes.punchline: He doesn't recognize them and introduces himself
jokes.explanation: Devastating character moment showing complete memory loss. Simple but powerfully effective moment.

jokes.text: Otlow had grown more erratic following post office closures last year by the Meyer Postal Commission.
jokes.setup: News about postal worker hoarding ballots
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Selina's own policies caused the ballot theft
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic callback showing how Selina's past 'achievements' created current problems. Excellent structure.

jokes.text: Can I not have just one minute to enjoy this? / I'll ask the Chinese
jokes.setup: Selina asking for a moment to celebrate
jokes.punchline: Deadpan offer to check with China about timing
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response treating rhetorical question literally with geopolitical twist

jokes.text: Thanks, buttfucker.
jokes.setup: Bob being shown to his new basement office
jokes.punchline: Crude parting shot to aide who helped him
jokes.explanation: Final Bob moment showing his complete loss of social filters. Crude but character-appropriate ending.
